items.claim: Roy Val is very active with 5.5 significant strikes per minute.
items.quote: Roy Val is very active. 5.5 significant strikes per minute

items.claim: Kavanaaugh has a 94% takedown defense rate.
items.quote: Kavanaaugh 94% takedown defense. That's impressive so far.

items.claim: Roy Val's 45% takedown defense is a vulnerability that Kavanaaugh can exploit.
items.quote: Roy Val's got a 45% takedown defense. So that might be something that Loner wants to attack.

items.claim: Cory Sanhagen's takedown defense percentage dropped to 56% after being taken down 20 times by Merab Dvalishvili.
items.quote: Morab Devalos really came in and took him down 20 times so it dropped that thing way back down to 56%.

items.claim: Mario Bautista has a slight edge in the takedown game over Cory Sanhagen.
items.quote: Bautista nearly two takedowns over three rounds. Sanhagen gets one every three. So, you know, slight edge for Bautista

items.claim: Patty Pimblett has a poor 44% takedown defense.
items.quote: the takedown defense of Patty the batty's important here, right? 44%. That's not very good.

items.claim: Max Holloway has a significant volume advantage in striking, averaging nearly seven significant strikes per minute compared to McGregor's 5.3.
items.quote: Holloway nearly seven significant strikes per minute. McGregor 5.3 per minute.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's lack of recent activity compared to Max Holloway favors Holloway.
items.quote: Holloway's still active, McGregor's not. That's got to favor Max Holloway.

items.claim: Conor McGregor will likely tire in the later rounds of the fight.
items.quote: I like Holloway to get the win. Probably by finish in the third or fourth round as McGregor gets tired
